steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate y
steps.content:
Add both equations:
$$(x^2 + y + 7) + (20x + 100 - y) = 7 + 0$$
The $y$ terms cancel, combining the constants:
$$x^2 + 20x + 107 = 7$$
Subtract 7 from both sides:
$$x^2 + 20x + 100 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor as a perfect square and solve
steps.content:
The expression $x^2 + 20x + 100$ is a perfect square trinomial:
$$(x + 10)^2 = 0$$
This gives $x + 10 = 0$, so $x = -10$.
